responsibilities.label: Identify Potential Candidates
responsibilities.description: Use professional network, community connections, and personal relationships to identify individuals who may benefit from Panchakarma — those with chronic conditions, lifestyle disorders, stress, metabolic issues, or seeking holistic rejuvenation.
activities: Screen potential candidates against the programme eligibility criteria
Initiate contact with interested individuals
Share orientation videos and materials to create awareness
Assess candidate motivation and readiness for the programme
Record candidate details using the standard referral checklist
responsibilities.boundary: Pracharakas must not make clinical recommendations or prognoses. Candidate identification is limited to interest and basic suitability assessment.

responsibilities.label: Educate on Ayurveda and Treatment Methods
responsibilities.description: Provide accurate, pre-approved information about Ayurveda principles, the Panchakarma treatment methodology, what the programme involves, and why it may be beneficial for the candidate.
activities: Share the SHIVA-approved orientation materials with prospective patients
Address general questions using the approved communication formats
Explain programme duration, schedule, and centre environment
Clarify the difference between general wellness and therapeutic Panchakarma
Set realistic expectations around outcomes and recovery
responsibilities.boundary: Pracharakas must use only SHIVA-approved materials for education. No promises of cure or clinical outcomes are to be made.

responsibilities.label: Create Patient Wish List Entry
responsibilities.description: For candidates who have expressed clear interest and meet the preliminary eligibility indicators, create a formal wish list entry in the Ayusurance system using the standard referral template.
activities: Complete the standard referral template for the candidate
Capture full name, contact details, location, chief complaint, and motivation
Record preferred programme duration and preferred months
Submit the wish list entry to the Ayusurance coordinator
Confirm receipt of the entry with the candidate
responsibilities.boundary: Only submit wish list entries for candidates who have expressed genuine interest and where basic eligibility indicators are met.

responsibilities.label: Assist During Screening Consultations
responsibilities.description: Support the patient during the screening consultation stage by ensuring they have the required documents, are prepared for the consultation, and understand the process.
activities: Remind the patient of the screening appointment
Assist the patient in gathering required medical documents
Clarify screening process questions from the patient
Maintain communication with the Ayusurance coordinator regarding patient status
Report any patient concerns or issues to the coordinator promptly
responsibilities.boundary: Pracharakas do not participate in or influence the clinical screening decision. Their role is coordination and patient support only.

responsibilities.label: Support the Patient Journey
responsibilities.description: Serve as an accessible point of contact and source of encouragement for the referred patient throughout their Panchakarma journey, from wish list to admission.
activities: Maintain periodic check-in communication with the referred patient
Escalate any patient concerns or hesitations to the Ayusurance coordinator
Encourage patient adherence to pre-programme guidelines
Celebrate patient milestones (reservation confirmed, departure, etc.)
Provide post-programme support guidance as directed by the coordinator
responsibilities.boundary: Pracharakas do not advise on clinical matters, medication, or treatment protocols at any point.
